page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.large is an entry-level instance belonging to the ecs.u1 server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor operating at 2.5 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on external storage solutions and utilizing a KVM hypervisor. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates moderate single-core capabilities but constrained multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yields an average score of 1834.58, and single-threaded gzip compression reaches 27.73 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to a poor rating of 1918.98, and the Passmark CPU score is similarly limited at 2716.28. Memory bandwidth is highly restricted, with large-block read and write speeds registering at 15.64 GB/sec and 13.95 GB/sec respectively, which places the memory subsystem in the bottom 10% of benchmarked hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u1-c1m1.large offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require high memory density or intensive parallel processing. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable single-threaded execution, but the 2.0 GB memory limit and poor multi-core scaling represent significant tradeoffs.

This server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic utility scripting, low-traffic static web serving, or minor background tasks. It is not recommended for database operations, large-scale LLM inference, or memory-intensive applications due to its constrained RAM and low memory bandwidth.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.large is an entry-level, x86_64 architecture instance within the ecs.u1 family, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor and 2.0 GB of RAM. Running on a KVM hypervisor, this server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1834.58 and stable single-threaded compression. However, its multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the lower percentiles, reflecting a highly resource-constrained profile. Qualitatively, this instance offers a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads where predictable single-threaded CPU execution is required but memory demands are minimal. It is best suited for basic utility scripts, low-traffic static web serving, and simple background processes rather than memory-intensive applications or databases.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.large is a budget-friendly x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, dedicated dual-core utility instance designed for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic background processing.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for lightweight utility scripting and low-traffic web serving
